Reasoning
Q1
Directions:In the given questions, assuming the
given statements to be true. Find which of the
given two conclusions number I and II is/are
definitely 
true 
and 
give 
your 
answer
accordingly.
 
Statement: D < B ≤ # = F < 9 < 0
Conclusions:
I. # < 0
II. B ≤ F
(A) Only conclusion I follows
(B) Only conclusion II follows
(C) Both conclusions I and II follow
(D) Neither conclusion I nor II follows
(E) Either conclusion I or II follows

Statement: E < S ≤ ∞ = % < Y < @

II. Y > E

II.G ≥ E

Q5
Directions: Study the questions based on the

Statement: X = B < N, B < O ≥ A, K ≤ O = M > B
Conclusions:
I. B < K
II. M > B
(A) Only conclusion I follows
(B) Only conclusion II follows

(C) Both conclusion I and II follow
(D) Neither conclusion I nor II follows

Directions: Study the questions based on the
information given below.
Statement: X = U < T, U < S ≥ I, K ≤ S = M > U
Conclusions:
I.U < K
II.M > K
(A) Only conclusion 1 follows
(B) Only conclusion 2 follows
(C) Either conclusion 1 nor 2 follows
(D) Neither conclusion 1 nor 2 follows
(E) If both conclusion I and conclusion II follows

Directions:Analyze the data carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.
Statements: C < N < R > S, N< P
Conclusions:
I. P > S
II. R > C
(A) If only conclusion I follows
(B) If only conclusion II follows
(C) If either conclusion I or II follows
(D) If neither conclusion I nor II follows

Directions:Analyze the data carefully and
answer the questions accordingly.

II. Y > W
(A) If only conclusion I follows
(B) If only conclusion II follows
(C) If either conclusion I or II follows
(D) If neither conclusion I nor II follows

Q10
Directions:In the following question assuming
the given statements to be true, find which of
the interpretation among given interpretation
is/are definitely true and then give your
reaction accordingly.
 
Statements: M < N = O > P, Q > R < M, S ≤ P
Conclusions:
I. Q > P
II. N > S
III. R < O
IV. R ≥ O
(A) None is true
(B) Only IV is true
(C) Both II and III are true
(D) Either I or III and IV are true
(E) All are true
Q11
Directions:In the following question assuming
the given statements to be true, find which of
the interpretation among given interpretation
is/are definitely true and then give your
reaction accordingly.

Statements: E ≥ U = D; R < A < F; W ≤ D; W > F
Conclusions:
I. U < R
II. E = W
III. E > W
(A) Only II is True 
(B) Only III is True
(C) Only I and II are True
(D) Either I or II is true
(E) Either II or III is true
Q12
DIrections:In the following question assuming
the given statements to be true, find which of
the interpretation among given interpretation
is/are definitely true and then give your
reaction accordingly.
 
Statements: T ≥ C ≥ F; E = A < D; X > T; D < F = T
Conclusions:
I. F < E
II. C = F
III. A > T
(A) Only I is True
(B) Only II is True
(C) Only III is True
(D) Only I and III are True
(E) None is true
Q13
Directions:In the following question assuming
the given statements to be true, find which of
the interpretation among given interpretation
is/are definitely true and then give your
reaction accordingly.
Statements: Z > Y ≥ X ≥ K; K = L ≥ M; Which of the
following are definitely true?
(A) X > L
(B) Z > L
(C) K = Z
(D) K < Y
(E) None of these
Q14
Directions:In the following question assuming
the given statements to be True, find which of
the conclusion among given conclusions is/are
definitely true and then give your answers
accordingly.
Statements: P < Q ≥ G; G ≥ I ≥ E; C ≤ P; C > U
Conclusions:
I. U > I
II. P ≤ E
(A) Both I and II follow
(B) Either I or II follows
(C) Only I follows
(D) Only II follows
(E) Neither I nor II follow

Hints & Solutions
Q1
Text Solution:
Ans: C
Solution: Given statement: D < B ≤ # = F < 9 < 0
Conclusions: I. # < 0 (True), (As, # = F < 9 < 0, so
the relationship between # and 0
is # < 0)
II. B ≤ F (True), (As, B ≤ # = F, so the relationship
between B and F is B ≤ F)
Therefore, both conclusion 1 and 2 follow
Hence, option c is the correct answer.
Q2
Text Solution:
Ans: C
Solution: Given statement:@ ≤ & ≤ 7 = % > 1 < #
Conclusions: I. 7 ≥ @ (True), (As,@ ≤ & ≤ 7, so the
relationship between 7 and @ is 7 ≥ @)
II. 7 > 1 (True), (As, 7 = % > 1, so the relationship
between 7 and 1 is 7 > 1)
Therefore, both conclusion I and II follow
Q3
Text Solution:
 Given statement: E< S ≤ ∞ = % < Y < @
Conclusions:
I. Y ≥ S (False), (As, S ≤ ∞ = % < Y, so the
relationship between Y and S is Y > S).
II. Y > E (True), (As, E < S ≤ ∞ = % < Y, so the
relationship between Y and E is Y > E)
Therefore, only conclusion II is true.
Hence, Option B is the correct answer.
Q4
Text Solution:
Ans: A
Solution: Given statement: P = G < Q, E < L ≥ U, K
≤ G = R > L
On combining the given statements, we get
K ≤ G = R > L ≥ U
I. Conclusions: I. E < Q (True), (As Q >
G = R > L > E, so the relationship between E and
Q is E < Q)
III. II. G ≥ E (False), (As G = R > L> E, so
the relationship between G and E is G > E)
Therefore, only conclusion I is true.
Hence, option a is the correct answer.
 
Q5
Text Solution:
Ans: B
Solution: Given statement: X = B < N, B < O ≥ A, K
≤ O = M > B
On combining the given statements, we get:
K ≤ O = M > B < O ≥ A
Conclusions:
I. B < K (False), (As: K ≤ O = M > B < O ≥ A, so the
relationship 
between 
B 
and 
K 
can’t 
be
determined) 
 II. M > B (True), (As: K ≤ O = M > B < O ≥ A, so the
relationship between M and B is M > B)
Therefore, only conclusion II is true.
Hence, option b is the correct answer.
Q6
Text Solution:
Given statement: X = U < T, U < S ≥ I, K ≤ S = M > U
On combining the given statements, we get:
K ≤ S = M > U < S ≥ I
Conclusions:
I. U < K (False), (As: K ≤ S = M > U, so the
relationship 
between 
U 
and 
K 
can’t 
be
determined)
I. II. M > K (False), (As: K ≤ S = M, so the
relationship between M and K is M > K)
Therefore, Neither conclusion 1 nor 2 follows
Hence, Option D is the correct answer.
Q7
Text Solution:
I. P > N < R > S it is false 
II. C < N < R it is true 
so option B is correct

Q8
Text Solution:
I. U < P > Q False 
II. Y ≥ F < P >Q = W False
so option D is correct
Q9
Text Solution:
I. I = J > K = L True
II. K = L ≤ M True
So the option E is correct
Q10
Text Solution:
Given Statements: M < N = O > P, Q > R < M, S ≤ P
On combining: M < N = O > P ≥ S, Q > R < M < N =
O > P
Conclusions:
I. Q > P → False (as Q > R < M < N = O > P →
Relation cannot be determined)
II. N > S → True (as N = O > P ≥ S→N>S)
III. R < O → True (as R < M < N = O → R < O)
IV. R ≥ O → False (as R < M < N = O → R < O)
Hence, the correct option is (C).
Q11
Text Solution:
Given statements: E ≥ U = D; R < A < F; W ≤ D; W >
F On combining: E ≥ U = D ≥ W > F > A > R
Conclusions:
I. U < R → False (as U = D ≥ W > F > A > R → U ≥ W
> R → U > R)
II. E = W → False (as E ≥ U = D ≥ W → E ≥ D ≥ W → E
≥ W)
III. E > W → False (as E ≥ U = D ≥ W → E ≥ D ≥ W →
E ≥ W)
Since, conclusion II and III form complementary
pair and E ≥ W.
Hence, the correct option is (E).
Q12
Text Solution:
Given statements: T ≥ C ≥ F; E = A < D; X > T; D < F
= T
On combining: E = A < D < F ≤ C ≤ T < X; F = T
Conclusions:
I. F < E → False (as E = A < D < F → E < F)
II. C = F → False (as per the given information F =
T & T ≥ C ≥ F)
III. A > T → False (as A < D < F ≤ C ≤ T → A < F ≤ C ≤
T → A < C ≤ T → A < T)
Hence, the correct option is (E).
Q13
Text Solution:
Given statements: Z > Y ≥ X ≥ K; K = L ≥ M; On
combining: Z > Y ≥ X ≥ K = L ≥ M;
Conclusions:
I. X > L → False (as X ≥ K and K = L implies X ≥ L,
thus a clear relation cannot be determined)
II. Z > L → True (as Z > Y ≥ X ≥ K; K = L implies Z >
L)
III. K = Z → False (as Z > Y ≥ X ≥ K implies K < Z)
IV. K < Y → False (as Z > Y ≥ X ≥ K implies Y ≥ K,
thus a clear relation cannot be determined)
Hence, the correct option is (B).
Q14
Text Solution:
Given statements: P < Q ≥ G; G ≥ I ≥ E; C ≤ P; C > U
On combining: U < C ≤ P < Q ≥ G ≥ I ≥ E
Conclusions:
I. U > I → False (as U < C ≤ P < Q ≥ G ≥ I → thus
clear relation between U and I cannot be
determined)
II. P ≤ E → False (as P < Q ≥ G ≥ I ≥ E → thus clear
relation between P and E cannot be determined)
Hence, neither I nor II is True.
